input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload support)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Discover & Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads (find exact maui workload name)
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls Android, Windows dependencies automatically)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK (via workload or manually)

The `maui` workload installs Android SDK components automatically. To check or manage them:

```powershell
# See what Android components were installed
dotnet workload list

# If you need standalone Android SDK management, install command-line tools:
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools

# Discover installed Android SDK paths
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidHome)) {
    # MAUI workload installs here instead:
    $androidHome = "$env:USERPROFILE\.android\sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK: $androidHome"

# List installed platforms/build-tools dynamically
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed
```

## 4. Accept Android Licenses

```powershell
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Install Windows App SDK (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Discover latest version
winget search "Windows App SDK"

# Install the runtime
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.1.7

# Enable Developer Mode (required for UWP/WinUI sideloading)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## 6. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (optional, but recommended)

```powershell
# Discover available VS versions
winget search Microsoft.VisualStudio

# Install Build Tools with MAUI + UWP components
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools --override `
    "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
     --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
     --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.MSIX.Packaging `
     --includeRecommended --quiet --wait"
```

## 7. Create & Verify a Project

```powershell
# List MAUI templates to confirm installation
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Run on Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available AVDs
& "$androidHome\emulator\emulator.exe" -list-avds

# Create one if none exist (discover system images first)
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "system-images"
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_API_34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Launch emulator
Start-Process "$androidHome\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_API_34"

# Deploy
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

---

**Key tips:**
- The `dotnet workload install maui` command is the single most important step — it pulls Android SDK, build tools, and platform dependencies automatically.
- Use `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
- If target framework versions change, run `dotnet new maui --list` and check the generated `.csproj` for the current TFM names.
